mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above its 12-month trend, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit margins. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100,000 persons month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market cooling signals in late 2022
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following payroll declines are not significantly negative (mean return > 0% with t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS adjusted for CPIAUCSL inflation) signal robust consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Real retail sales growth does not predict positive 20-day SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.05, or correlation between real RSAFS momentum and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below its 6-month moving average signal supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, constraining corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average by > 1.0 index point
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,200 thousand units annualized while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 4.0%, signaling joint demand destruction in residential investment and labor market stress that reduces equity risk appetite and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean > 0% with t-test p > 0.10) when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate from its recent peak signals monetary policy pivot toward easing that reduces equity discount rates and improves refinancing conditions for corporations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle or policy pivot
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window during easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5% while nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) contracts, signaling accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and corporate hiring expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: recession onset or late-cycle labor market shock
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean > 0% with t-test p > 0.10) when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 1.5% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) contract month-over-month, signaling demand-side recession risk that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 1.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean > 0% with t-test p > 0.10) when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
